Title:
Verify that GET /products returns 200 OK and only in-stock products when 
available query parameter is set to true
Pre-conditions: Products exist in the inventory with both inStock=true and inStock=false.
Test Execution Steps:
1. Send a GET request to /products with available query parameter set to true.
2. Retrieve the response.
3. Verify the HTTP status code.
4. Iterate through each product in the response array and check its inStock status.
Expected Result:
• HTTP Status: 200 OK
• Response body contains a JSON array of products.
• Every product in the array has "inStock": true.

Title: Verify that GET /products returns 200 OK and only out-of-stock products when available query parameter is set to false
Pre-conditions: Products exist in the inventory with both inStock=true and inStock=false.
Test Execution Steps:
1. Send a GET request to /products with available query parameter set to false.
2. Retrieve the response.
3. Verify the HTTP status code.
4. Iterate through each product in the response array and check its inStock status.
Expected Result:
• HTTP Status: 200 OK
• Response body contains a JSON array of products.
• Every product in the array has "inStock": false.

Title: Verify that GET /products returns 200 OK and products belonging to the specified category when category query parameter is provided
Pre-conditions: Products exist in the inventory for all valid categories (meat-seafood, fresh-produce, candy, bread-bakery, dairy, eggs, coffee).
Test Execution Steps:
1. Send a GET request to /products with category query parameter set to a valid category (e.g., coffee).
2. Retrieve the response.
3. Verify the HTTP status code.
4. Iterate through each product in the response and verify its category.
Expected Result:
• HTTP Status: 200 OK
• Response body contains a JSON array of products.
• Every product in the array has its category property match the requested category.

Title: Verify that GET /products returns 200 OK and limits the number of products to the value of results query parameter when results parameter is between 1 and 20
Pre-conditions: At least 5 products exist in the inventory.
Test Execution Steps:
1. Send a GET request to /products with results query parameter set to 5.
2. Retrieve the response.
3. Verify the HTTP status code.
4. Count the number of products in the returned array.
Expected Result:
• HTTP Status: 200 OK
• Response body contains a JSON array of products.
• The size of the returned array is less than or equal to 5.

Title: Verify that GET /products returns 200 OK and filters products by category, availability, and limit when category, available, and results query parameters are provided
Pre-conditions: Products exist in the inventory matching the category and availability filters.
Test Execution Steps:
1. Send a GET request to /products with query parameters: category=fresh-produce, available=true, and results=3.
2. Retrieve the response.
3. Verify the HTTP status code.
4. Verify the size of the returned array and check the attributes of each product.
Expected Result:
• HTTP Status: 200 OK
• Response body contains a JSON array of products.
• The size of the returned array is less than or equal to 3.
• Every product in the array has "category": "fresh-produce" and "inStock": true.

Title: Verify that GET /products returns 200 OK and zero products when results query parameter is set to 0
Test Execution Steps:
1. Send a GET request to /products with results query parameter set to 0.
2. Retrieve the response.
3. Verify the HTTP status code.
4. Verify the size of the returned array.
Expected Result:
• HTTP Status: 200 OK
• Response body contains an empty JSON array [].

Title: Verify that GET /products returns 400 Bad Request and validation error when an invalid category query parameter is provided
Test Execution Steps:
1. Send a GET request to /products with category query parameter set to "invalid-category".
2. Retrieve the response.
3. Verify the HTTP status code.
4. Verify the error message details in the response body.
Expected Result:
• HTTP Status: 400 Bad Request
• Response body contains an error object: {"error": "Invalid value for query parameter 'category'"}

Title: Verify that GET /products returns 400 Bad Request and validation error when results query parameter is below 0 (e.g., -1)
Test Execution Steps:
1. Send a GET request to /products with results query parameter set to -1.
2. Retrieve the response.
3. Verify the HTTP status code.
4. Verify the error message details in the response body.
Expected Result:
• HTTP Status: 400 Bad Request
• Response body contains an error object: {"error": "Invalid value for query parameter 'results'"}

Title: Verify that GET /products returns 400 Bad Request and validation error when results query parameter exceeds 20 (e.g., 1000)
Test Execution Steps:
1. Send a GET request to /products with results query parameter set to 1000.
2. Retrieve the response.
3. Verify the HTTP status code.
4. Verify the error message details in the response body.
Expected Result:
• HTTP Status: 400 Bad Request
• Response body contains an error object: {"error": "Invalid value for query parameter 'results'"}

Title: Verify that GET /products/{productId} returns 200 OK and product details when a valid product ID is requested
Pre-conditions: A valid product exists in the inventory.
Test Execution Steps:
1. Retrieve a valid product from the inventory to obtain its ID.
2. Send a GET request to /products/{productId} using that valid ID.
3. Retrieve the response.
4. Verify the HTTP status code.
5. Deserialize the response and compare the fields (id, category, name, inStock) with the expected product details.
Expected Result:
• HTTP Status: 200 OK
• Response body contains a single product object matching the requested product ID.
• The product object has correct id, category, name, and inStock details.

Title: Verify that GET /products/{productId} returns 404 Not Found and error message when a non-existent product ID is requested
Test Execution Steps:
1. Define a non-existent product ID (e.g., 9999).
2. Send a GET request to /products/9999.
3. Retrieve the response.
4. Verify the HTTP status code.
5. Verify the error message details in the response body.
Expected Result:
• HTTP Status: 404 Not Found
• Response body contains an error object: {"error": "No product with id 9999"}
